article_title: Assessing in vivo flexion-extension quality of motion after cervical disc arthroplasty: a pilot study
label: Figure 1
caption: Centre of rotation.

article_title: Assessing in vivo flexion-extension quality of motion after cervical disc arthroplasty: a pilot study
label: Figure 2
caption: Centre of rotation in (A) C5-C6 and (B) C6-C7 arthroplasty group. (0,0) denotes the centre of superior endplate of caudal vertebra. COR, centre of rotation.

article_title: Assessing in vivo flexion-extension quality of motion after cervical disc arthroplasty: a pilot study
label: Figure 3
caption: Average motion fraction throughout the arc of flexion-extension.

article_title: Assessing in vivo flexion-extension quality of motion after cervical disc arthroplasty: a pilot study
label: Figure 4
caption: Segmental motion versus global range of motion. FE, flexion-extension; ROM, range of motion.

article_title: Traumatic Migration of the Bryan Cervical Disc Arthroplasty
label: Fig. 1
caption: Anteroposterior (A) and lateral (B) preoperative radiographs demonstrating spondylosis and anterior osteophyte formation at C5–C6.

article_title: Traumatic Migration of the Bryan Cervical Disc Arthroplasty
label: Fig. 2
caption: Sequential sagittal magnetic resonance image slices (A, B) demonstrating disk–osteophyte complex resulting in moderate central canal narrowing with moderate left and mild right neural foraminal stenosis at C5–C6.

article_title: Traumatic Migration of the Bryan Cervical Disc Arthroplasty
label: Fig. 3
caption: Anteroposterior (A) and lateral (B) immediate postoperative radiographs demonstrating well-positioned and appropriately sized single-level Bryan Cervical Disc arthroplasty device at C5-C6.

article_title: Traumatic Migration of the Bryan Cervical Disc Arthroplasty
label: Fig. 4
caption: Lateral (A), flexion (B), and extension (C) radiographs at 6 weeks postoperatively demonstrating no change in the location or placement of the device, without evidence of migration or subsidence.

article_title: Traumatic Migration of the Bryan Cervical Disc Arthroplasty
label: Fig. 5
caption: Anteroposterior (A) and lateral (B) radiographs at 3 months postoperation demonstrating no change in position of the implant.

article_title: Traumatic Migration of the Bryan Cervical Disc Arthroplasty
label: Fig. 6
caption: Anteroposterior (A), lateral (B), flexion (C), and extension (D) radiographs at 6 months postoperatively showing migration of the Bryan Cervical Disc device ∼2 mm anteriorly, without change in implant position on dynamic radiography.
